headline: Israel floor confirmation. UN Assistant Secretary-General Khiari described Gaza ceasefire as 'increasingly fragile' on April 28. Approximately 800 Palestinians killed since ceasefire began including 200+ children and 7 humanitarian workers. 1.8 million people displaced and aid-dependent. ICJ genocide case proceedings continuing. 'Dual-use' restrictions continue to limit UN humanitarian response. Floor maintained.
key_evidence: April 28 2026: UN ASG Khiari described Gaza ceasefire as 'increasingly fragile.'
Since ceasefire began: ~800 Palestinians killed including 200+ children and 7 humanitarian workers (UN reporting).
Critical humanitarian items classified as 'dual-use' continue to restrict UN response capacity.
ICJ genocide case: Israel submitted response in March 2026 after two delays; South Africa's case ongoing.
Hamas disarmament talks have not produced agreement.

evidence.finding: ASG Khiari: Gaza ceasefire 'increasingly fragile'; Israeli strikes and Hamas armed activities continue.
~800 Palestinians killed since ceasefire began including 200+ children and 7 humanitarian workers.
Monthly forecast on Middle East/Palestinian question; ICJ proceedings; ceasefire fragility.

scoring_rationale.EQU: 1 unchanged (floor). Dual-use restrictions disproportionately affect aid-dependent population.
scoring_rationale.BND: 1 unchanged (floor). Ceasefire fragility and Hamas disarmament impasse sustain floor-level B3.

negativeSignals: Ceasefire 'increasingly fragile' per UN ASG; ~800 deaths since ceasefire including 200+ children and 7 aid workers; 1.8M displaced; dual-use restrictions; ICJ genocide proceedings continuing.
positiveSignals: ICJ submission filed in March (after two delays) — process compliance, not substantive shift.
sector_context: Floor-cluster maintained. ICJ proceedings continue alongside the floor designation; verdict-pending evidence not yet scored.
open_actions_resolved: Ceasefire fragility scored as compound negative ACT/AWR notation
open_actions_carried: ICJ submission content and proceedings: monitor for future cycles
Floor exit criteria require restoration of humanitarian access, cessation of strikes, and accountability mechanism
